paper_title: Moving to the Adjacent Possible: Discovering Paths for Export Diversification in Rwanda
research_answer: Rwanda's export basket is extraordinarily concentrated. Just three commodities — coffee, tea, and tin — have historically accounted for more than 80% of the country's total exports, and these same products continue to drive the bulk of export growth today. This extreme concentration is reflected in Rwanda having comparative advantage (RCA > 1) in fewer than 40 products as of 2010, and an average Product Complexity Index of -0.95, meaning the products Rwanda exports are roughly one standard deviation below the world average in complexity. With exports per capita below $50 — one of the lowest levels globally — Rwanda's trade profile is characteristic of a heavily commodity-dependent economy with very limited diversification.
supporting_quotes: Three basic commodities – coffee, tea, and tin – made up more than 80 percent of the country's exports through its history and still drive the bulk of export growth today.
at less than US$50 per capita, Rwanda still has one of the lowest levels of exports in the world
As of 2010, Rwanda had RCA in less than 40 products across a number of communities.

Eliminate all products with a PCI below the existing average PCI of the products that Rwanda's exports (which is -0.95, meaning that on average, products exported by Rwanda are roughly one standard deviation below the average PCI of all products exported by countries in the world).
